feed.title: Petr's Blog
feed.subtitle: Blogging about DevOps, Kubernets, RPi, tips, ...

entry.title: Build secure and cheap Amazon EKS

link.title: Build secure and cheap Amazon EKS

entry.summary: I will outline the steps for setting up an Amazon EKS environment that is cost-effective while prioritizing security, and include standard applications in the configuration. Amazon EKS should align with these cost-effective criteria: Two AZ, use one zone if possible (less payments for cross AZ traffic) Spot instances Less expensive region - us-east-1 Most price efficient EC2 instance...

entry.title: Build secure Amazon EKS with Cilium and network encryption

link.title: Build secure Amazon EKS with Cilium and network encryption

entry.summary: I’m going to describe how to install Amazon EKS with Karpenter and Cilium (+ standard apps). Amazon EKS should meet these “cheap” requirements: Two AZ only - less payments for cross availability zones traffic Spot instances Less expensive region - us-east-1 Most price efficient EC2 instance type t4g.medium (2 x CPU, 4GB RAM) using AWS Graviton based on ARM Use Bottlerocket OS - sma...

entry.title: My favourite krew plugins for kubectl

link.title: My favourite krew plugins for kubectl

entry.summary: I would like to share few notes about kubectl plugins installed by krew which I’m using… It should not be comprehensive description of plugins, but I prefer to focus on the examples and screenshots. Links: Suman Chakraborty’s Post Top 15 Kubectl plugins for security engineers Kubernetes: Krew plugins manager, and useful kubectl plugins list Making Kubernetes Operations Easy with kube...

entry.title: Secrets Store CSI Driver and Reloader

link.title: Secrets Store CSI Driver and Reloader

entry.summary: Sometimes it is necessary to store the secrets in the HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault or others and use them in Kubernetes. In this post I would like to look at the way how to store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager retrieve them using Kubernetes Secrets Store CSI Driver with AWS Secrets and Configuration Provider (ASCP) and use them as Kubernetes Secret and also mount them ...

entry.title: Velero and cert-manager
entry.summary: In the previous post related to Cheapest Amazon EKS I’m using the cert-manager to get the wildcard certificate for the ingress. When the Let’s Encrypt production certificates are used, it may be handy to backup and restore them when the cluster is recreated. Here are few steps how to install Velero and backup + restore procedure for the cert-manager objects. Links: Backup and Restore Res...
